title: Beirut strike kills four as IDF targets Quds Force commanders
description: An Israeli airstrike on an apartment building in central Beirut has killed at least four people and injured ten others.  The building was reportedly housing displaced people.  Israel's military released a statement saying it targeted key commanders from Iran's Quds Force operating in Lebanon.

title: 'Whatever it takes': Hume leaves door open to One Nation deal
description: Liberal Deputy Leader Jane Hume claims her party should adopt a "whatever it takes" election strategy, leaving the door open for a deal with One Nation.  Ms Hume says the party's aim is to win and retain government at the next election.  Speaking to the Daily Telegraph, she announced Liberals should allocate their preferences on a seat-by-seat basis.

title: US military rejects Iranian claim of imprisoned American soldiers
description: The US Central Command has denied that any US service members had been taken hostage or captured by Iran.  This comes after Iranian Security Chief Ali Larijani announced several American soldiers had been imprisoned.  According to US Navy Captain Tim Hawkins, the Iranian leadership is doing everything possible to spread lies and deception.  He claims, "This is yet another clear example".

title: Government has initiated additional disaster recovery funding for NT after severe flooding
description: Emergency Management Minister Kristy McBain says the government has initiated “additional” disaster recovery funding arrangements for the Northern Territory after severe flooding.  Ms McBain told Sky News Australia that it includes personal hardship payments and reestablishment of white goods.  “And those things that may be lost as part of this.”

title: ‘Pleasing’ to see Australia has sufficient fuel reserves amid oil shortages
description: Emergency Management Minister Kristy McBain says it’s “important” to make sure there are fuel reserves for Australians.  Ms McBain told Sky News Australia that it’s “pleasing” to see we have the highest amount of fuel reserves in 15 years.  “Having a strategic reserve in the US, which was what the former government had, doesn’t work for us here.”

title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was regarded as the ‘architect’ for oppression in Iran
description: Sky News host Jaimee Rogers says Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was widely regarded as the “architect” of decades of oppression in Iran.  Ms Rogers said the regime has funded militant groups “across the region”.  “Violently suppressing its own citizens and exporting instability beyond its borders.”

title: Majority of Iranians are ‘extremely angry’ towards the Islamic regime
description: Journalist and Author Tara Kangarlou says ordinary civilians are the “heart” of the conflict in the Middle East.  Escalating tension in the Middle East continues to raise serious questions about regional stability and what comes next.  Ms Kangarlou told Sky News host Jaimee Rogers that the majority of the Iranian public is “extremely angry” towards the Islamic regime.

title: ‘Evident’ that Iranians want to get away from the ‘dictatorship’ leading the nation
description: Journalist and Author Tara Kangarlou says the Iranian people viewed the Islamic regime as a “dictatorship”.  Ms Kangarlou told Sky News host Jaimee Rogers that Iranians wanted to move “beyond” the regime.  “And that sentiment in Iran is wide and is evident.”

title: Iranian citizens ‘paying the price’ for the conflict
description: Journalist and Author Tara Kangarlou says the “hell” in Iran continues as the US and Israel continue their attacks.  Ms Kangarlou told Sky News host Jaimee Rogers that it’s “dangerous” for the Iranian people to be amongst the strikes.  “At its core, you have ordinary civilians who are paying the price at a time that the economy in Iran is essentially in freefall.”

title: Donald Trump has ‘admired’ the Royal Family even before he became president
description: Royal Journalist and Broadcaster Pandora Forsyth says US President Donald Trump has “admired” the UK Royal Family for many years.  Ms Forsyth told Sky News host Jaimee Rogers that Donald Trump had praised the Royals “before” he even joined the political world.  “And even more so now that he is in the White House.”

title: Iranian women’s football team ‘under house arrest’ after refusing to sing regime’s anthem
description: Sky News host James Macpherson says the Iranians women’s football team is “under house arrest” after refusing to sing the regime’s national anthem.  Mr Macpherson said a commentator on Iranian state TV labelled them “traitors” to the nation.  “It’s amazing how having state TV call for your imprisonment or worse, changes one’s demeanour.”

title: UK ‘preparing’ to send aircraft carrier into the Middle East
description: Sky News host Paul Murray says the United Kingdom is “preparing” to send one of its aircraft carriers into the Middle East.  Mr Murray said it will be there “in case they need to fire” into Iran.  “If British assets are attacked.”

title: Labor pushes for ‘stronger rules’ around polling booths after reports of aggressive behaviour
description: Sky News host Paul Murray says there was supposedly a “new level of abuse” happening at the 2025 election as people were going into polling stations.  Mr Murray said the Labor Party is pushing for “stronger rules” around polling booths.  “After reports of aggressive and intimidating behaviour towards volunteers and voters during that election.”

title: Keir Starmer has not ‘stepped up’ to go ‘toe to toe’ with the US in the Middle East
description: On tonight’s episode of Paul Murray Live, Sky News host Paul Murray discusses the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, Donald Trump’s lashing out at Keir Starmer and more.

title: One Nation candidate vows to deal with ‘real issues’ facing Farrer electorate
description: One Nation candidate for Farrer David Farley says there are some “real issues” in his electorate.  Mr Farley told Sky News host Paul Murray that Farrer has been “underrepresented” for about 25 years.  “Right at the core of it is water.”
